slice: direct Rust public re-export lookup and pricing
goal: Follow one direct same-corpus Rust public re-export only when it resolves to an already literal-safe provider export, and price non-value re-export targets separately from generic module/export misses.
implemented_capabilities: Rust lowering now emits first-party ReExportBinding evidence for direct public use declarations, including visibility-qualified pub(crate) forms and direct brace aliases.
Imported immutable snapshot lookup follows one same-corpus Rust re-export hop only when the target resolves to a unique direct literal-safe export.

Rust same-crate module identity now recognizes bare child module aliases such as context::Item from a parent module file, which moves many previously generic module misses into concrete non-value boundaries.
The import snapshot census now prices direct re-export targets separately as callable, type, and unresolved target-export boundaries.
closed_boundaries: private use declarations do not emit re-export proof
wildcard and nested brace re-exports do not emit direct re-export proof
re-exports to callable/type/module namespace targets stay closed
re-export target module/export misses stay reported instead of admitting raw coordinates
notes: The crates corpus has no newly eligible literal-safe re-exported provider value in this slice, so snapshot_records stays 1. Focused tests cover the positive one-hop pub use literal export path.
The useful corpus movement is attribution quality: generic module/export target rows moved 139 -> 91, and provider-export-missing moved 9 -> 2.

Admission rejections increased by 4 because the focused Rust tests add new helper units; the increase is attributed to existing import-symbol and receiver-domain buckets, with false merges and canon preservation unchanged.
